apps.name: apple-health-connector
apps.title: Apple Health Connector
apps.description: Ports over, collects, and analyzes data from Apple Health
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in to the dashboard; the health-data ingest endpoint is gated by a self-issued API key rather than OpenHost SSO.

apps.name: claude-workbench
apps.title: Claude Workbench
apps.description: Tabbed in-browser terminals preloaded with Claude Code and a cloned openhost repo, for building and debugging openhost apps.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in to the terminal via OpenHost SSO; API keys and the login state persist across redeploys, but it is single-owner only with no guest access.

apps.name: code-completion
apps.title: Code Completion
apps.description: Code completion server with model management UI and OpenAI-compatible API.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged into the management UI via the router (no login form); the OpenAI-compatible API is public and gated by an app-issued token.

apps.name: filestash
apps.title: Filestash
apps.description: A powerful web-based file management system, compatible with SFTP, S3, and Google Drive
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Owner is auto-logged into the file manager with zero clicks via passthrough auth; public share links aren't exposed, so they require owner login too.

apps.name: forgejo
apps.title: Forgejo
apps.description: Self-hosted Git forge with issues, pull requests, CI/CD, and package registry. OpenHost zone owner is auto-logged in as admin; invited users get Forgejo-local password accounts.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in as admin; invited users still create Forgejo-local password accounts.

apps.name: gemini
apps.title: Gemini
apps.description: Agate-based Gemini protocol server. Browse the capsule with any Gemini client over a self-signed TLS connection on port 1965.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Serves a Gemini capsule over its own TLS on port 1965; the protocol has no OpenHost-style web SSO to adopt.

apps.name: graphhopper
apps.title: GraphHopper
apps.description: Self-hosted routing engine with car, bike, and foot navigation. Includes a map UI with turn-by-turn directions and geocoding.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Stateless routing engine gated by OpenHost zone auth with an owner-only region admin; no accounts to manage and nothing to leak.

apps.name: hidden-service
apps.title: Tor Hidden Service
apps.description: Tor hidden service with a simple page editor CMS.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Runs and persists pages correctly, but the editor does no OpenHost SSO of its own; owner access relies on the router gating the admin web port. The public capsule is anonymous over Tor.

apps.name: jitsi
apps.title: Jitsi Meet
apps.description: Scalable video conferencing with an SFU (Jitsi Videobridge) — anyone with the URL can join a meeting.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Anyone with the meeting URL can join by design; no per-user OpenHost login, which fits the conferencing model.

apps.name: minecraft
apps.title: Minecraft
apps.description: A self-hosted minecraft server runner
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Server provisions and persists worlds natively; players authenticate via Minecraft accounts, as expected for a game server.

apps.name: miniflux
apps.title: Miniflux
apps.description: Minimalist RSS/Atom reader with bundled PostgreSQL and OpenHost single sign-on.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in via OpenHost SSO; bundled Postgres persists feeds cleanly.

apps.name: mirotalk-p2p
apps.title: MiroTalk P2P
apps.description: Peer-to-peer video conferencing. Browsers connect directly; the server only handles signaling, never media. OpenHost zone owner is auto-logged in as host.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in as host; guests join via the room URL. Signaling-only, no media stored.

apps.name: navidrome
apps.title: Navidrome
apps.description: Modern music server and streamer compatible with Subsonic/Airsonic clients.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Owner is auto-logged in to the music server; libraries persist correctly under the app data dir.

apps.name: onionshare
apps.title: OnionShare
apps.description: Anonymously share files, host a website, or run a chat room over Tor hidden services.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Owner manages shares; recipients use the generated Tor onion link anonymously, so no web SSO applies to guests.

apps.name: openclaw
apps.title: OpenClaw
apps.description: OpenClaw AI assistant.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Owner reaches the assistant with OpenHost auth in front, but invited users need app-local setup.

apps.name: openweb-ui
apps.title: Open WebUI
apps.description: Self-hosted ChatGPT-style interface for local and remote LLMs (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc).
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner lands auto-logged-in as admin (Open WebUI auth disabled, router-gated); guests are bounced to the zone login. Nothing to set up.

apps.name: oura-connector
apps.title: Oura Connector
apps.description: Ports over, collects, and analyzes data from Oura fitness rings
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Runs and persists data correctly, but there is no OpenHost SSO; access relies on the router gating the app, and the OAuth setup page is not owner-checked in-app.

apps.name: pihole
apps.title: Pi-hole
apps.description: Network-wide DNS ad blocker. Blocks ads and trackers at the DNS level for all devices on your network. OpenHost zone owner is auto-logged in to the admin dashboard.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in to the admin dashboard, but it sits in front of Pi-hole's own session, leaving some rough edges.

apps.name: redlib
apps.title: Redlib
apps.description: Private front-end for Reddit. Browse posts and comments without JavaScript, ads, tracking, or a Reddit account; all requests are proxied through the server.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Stateless public Reddit front-end; no accounts to manage and nothing to leak, so it integrates cleanly.

apps.name: searxng
apps.title: SearXNG
apps.description: Privacy-respecting metasearch engine that aggregates results from 70+ search services.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Stateless public metasearch; no accounts to manage and nothing to leak, so it integrates cleanly.

apps.name: synapse
apps.title: Matrix Synapse
apps.description: Reference homeserver for the Matrix decentralized communication protocol. End-to-end encrypted messaging.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Runs and persists data correctly, but there is no OpenHost SSO; you self-register and sign in with Synapse-local credentials in your Matrix client.

apps.name: uptime-kuma
apps.title: Uptime Kuma
apps.description: Self-hosted uptime monitoring with HTTP, TCP, DNS, and ping checks plus status pages.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Owner is auto-logged in to the dashboard, but invited viewers fall back to Uptime Kuma's own login.

apps.name: vaultwarden
apps.title: VaultWarden
apps.description: A community developed password manager server, compatible with BitWarden's client
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Runs and persists data correctly, but there is no OpenHost SSO; the owner signs in through Vaultwarden's own email and master-password login form.

apps.name: webtop
apps.title: Webtop
apps.description: Full XFCE Linux desktop in the browser. Everything under ~ (browser profiles, proot-apps installs, downloads, shell history) persists across app restarts; apt-installed system packages do not.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Desktop session persists under the home dir, but you reach it through the app's own gate rather than OpenHost auto-login.

apps.name: wg-easy
apps.title: wg-easy
apps.description: WireGuard VPN server with a web UI. Connect any device (Linux, macOS, iOS, Android, Windows) back to your zone over the public internet.
apps.openhost_integration_score_explanation: Zone owner is auto-logged in to the web UI; VPN peers authenticate with WireGuard keys, as expected.
